display.purpose: Page-level h1, used once per screen.

shadows.name: ambient-card
shadows.purpose: Default resting shadow for cards, modals, tutorial coach marks.

shadows.name: button-hover
shadows.purpose: Button lift on hover, paired with translateY(-2px) scale(1.02).

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.value: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent), transparent 65%), 0 0 0 8px color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent), transparent 87%)

shadows.name: glass-panel
shadows.purpose: Deepest shadow, reserved for full-viewport gate moments (auth gate).

shadows.name: inset-highlight
shadows.purpose: Thin top-edge sheen on glass and elevated surfaces.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for button/card transform transitions.

motion.name: button-float
motion.purpose: Idle floating animation on buttons at rest; disabled under performance-mode/ios-optimized.

motion.name: card-pop-in
motion.purpose: Entrance animation for cards and modals.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action pill button (e.g. Clean Notes).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background-image: linear-gradient(120deg, #3557ff 0%, #5c8bff 45%, #2847df 100%); background-size: 180% 100%; color: #ffffff; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 999px; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; padding: 0.75rem 1.2rem; transition: transform 0.34s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 0.3s, filter 0.25s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px) scale(1.02); box-shadow: 0 10px 22px rgba(40, 61, 158, 0.2); filter: brightness(1.03); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(53,87,255,0.35), 0 0 0 8px rgba(53,87,255,0.13); outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0) scale(0.97); box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(40, 61, 158, 0.16); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: linear-gradient(180deg, #f2f4fb 0%, #d8dfef 100%); color: #1d2240; border: 1px solid #c3cbe6; border-radius: 999px; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; padding: 0.45rem 0.9rem; transition: transform 0.34s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 0.3s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px) scale(1.02); box-shadow: 0 10px 22px rgba(40, 61, 158, 0.2); }

components.name: Default Card
components.description: Base surface card used across the app for notes/results/tasks.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #d9def5; border-radius: 18px; box-shadow: 0 16px 40px rgba(40, 61, 158, 0.14); padding: 1rem 1.2rem; color: #1d2240; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; }

components.name: Text Input
components.css: .ds-input { background: transparent; border: 1px solid #d9def5; border-radius: 12px; padding: 0.8rem; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #1d2240; } .ds-input:focus { outline: 2px solid rgba(53,87,255,0.45); }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Accent-tinted uppercase badge (mode chip, progress pill, quiz badge).
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-block; background: rgba(53,87,255,0.1); color: #2847df; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.3rem 0.75rem; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.78rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Pro Badge
components.description: Gold gradient badge for Pro/God Mode gated features — the only place gold appears.
components.css: .ds-pro-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.25rem; background: linear-gradient(180deg, #ffe7a8 0%, #ffc86c 100%); border: 1px solid rgba(184,122,16,0.55); color: #7a4b00; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.2rem 0.65rem; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 0.75rem; }

narrative.overview: AI Study Planner exists for one moment: a student mid-cram, notes everywhere, pastes the mess into one box and hits Clean Notes. Everything the interface does should serve that moment — reduce the visual noise around the student, not add to it. The system is a soft indigo-blue command surface: airy off-white by day, deep navy by night, with a single trustworthy accent color doing almost all of the work. Motion is alive but gentle — buttons idle-float, hover shimmers sweep, flashcards flip in 3D — but it's already adaptive: performance-mode and ios-optimized strip it back for constrained devices, so the richness never costs reliability. That adaptivity is the calm: the system never makes the stressed user wait on an animation. This system explicitly rejects manufactured urgency — no countdown timers, no red alarm-toned upsells, no dense dashboard chrome competing for attention. Where gating exists (Pro, God Mode, the study-launch reveal-gate), it's presented as a soft, honest pause — a blurred preview and a clear CTA — never a dark pattern.
keyCharacteristics: One accent (trustworthy indigo), used deliberately, not scattered
Pill-shaped buttons and chips throughout — soft, approachable, never sharp
Soft-lifted cards on a diffuse indigo-tinted shadow, not flat, not heavy
Glass/blur reserved for high-stakes moments: the auth gate, the study-launch reveal
Motion is default-on but adaptive: idle float + shimmer at full fidelity, gracefully degrading under performance-mode/ios-optimized
Light and dark themes are full siblings, not an afterthought — every token has a dark counterpart

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Indigo is the only saturated color allowed outside status/gating contexts. Green, amber, coral, and gold each have exactly one job (success, warning, error, Pro) and never substitute for the primary accent.

rules.name: The Weighted-Not-Sized Rule
rules.body: Emphasis in this system comes from font-weight (600/700/800) far more than from size jumps — most UI text lives in a narrow 0.85–1.15rem band. Reach for weight before reaching for a bigger size.

rules.name: The Reserved Glass Rule
rules.body: Blur and translucency are for pause-moments (auth, paywall reveal) only. Everyday cards and buttons stay opaque with a soft ambient shadow — glass is not the default surface treatment, it's a signal.

dos: Do keep indigo as the only saturated accent outside status/gating (green/amber/coral/gold each have exactly one job).
Do use pill shape (999px) for every button, chip, and badge — it's the system's signature silhouette.
Do lead emphasis with font-weight, not font-size — most UI text lives in a narrow 0.85–1.15rem band.
Do reserve glass/blur for genuine pause-moments (auth gate, reveal-gates) — it signals 'the product is deliberately pausing you here.'
Do keep idle motion (float, shimmer) adaptive — respect performance-mode and ios-optimized so richness never costs reliability or accessibility.
Do give every light-theme token a dark-theme counterpart before shipping a new surface.
donts: Don't introduce manufactured urgency — no countdown timers, no red alarm-toned upsell banners, no scarcity language in Pro/God Mode gating.
Don't use gold anywhere except the Pro/God Mode badge; its rarity is what makes it register as a reward.
Don't apply glass/blur as a default card treatment — it's reserved for gate moments, not decoration.
Don't use border-left/border-right colored stripes as an accent device anywhere in this system.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text + gradient) for emphasis — this system's emphasis tool is weight, not gradient.
Don't stack more than one status color in the same component; a card is either neutral, success, warning, or error, never mixed.
